response.justification: Bogdan is a strong generalist engineer with an ETH Zürich CS degree and 16+ years of software development, but he falls significantly short of the role's core requirements. The position explicitly demands 8+ years building AI-driven solutions; his AI-specific work is confined almost entirely to roughly 13 months at re:cinq (April 2025–present), where it is described at a high level without naming concrete frameworks, models, or measurable outcomes. Python — the dominant language in the AI/ML ecosystem and a listed key technology — does not appear anywhere in his skills or employment history. There is no mention of vector databases, RAG architectures, LLM evaluation pipelines, or multi-agent frameworks such as LangGraph or CrewAI, all of which are central to the role. His data-pipeline experience (Dunnhumby) and MCP work at re:cinq are genuine positives, but they are insufficient to offset the breadth of gaps against a staff-level, customer-facing GenAI position.
strengths: ETH Zürich CS degree combined with 16+ years of polyglot engineering demonstrates a strong technical foundation
Direct hands-on experience with Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers and agentic-workflow infrastructure at re:cinq aligns with a stated key technology
Background in high-throughput data integration at Dunnhumby and geo-data pipeline work at GISCollective shows data-engineering aptitude
concerns: Python is absent from skills and all role descriptions — a critical gap given it is the primary language of the AI/ML ecosystem and explicitly required
No evidence of RAG architectures, vector databases, LLM evaluation/observability pipelines, or named multi-agent frameworks anywhere in the resume
AI-specific experience is limited to ~13 months at re:cinq, far below the 8-year minimum the role requires for production-grade AI solution development

key_factors.factor: Strong general engineering foundation and MCP exposure
